channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: tech

item.title: 3 years into war with Russia, this Ukrainian startup is powering a drone revolution
item.description: Ukraine’s war with Russia—sparked by Russia’s invasion in the spring of 2022—is now entering its fourth year. So too is Sine.Engineering, a company born amid the conflict.
item.dc:creator: Chris Stokel-Walker, Fast Company

item.title: With a TikTok ban looming, Trump signals a deal will come before April 5 deadline
item.description: As the deadline to strike a deal over TikTok approaches this week, President Donald Trump has signaled that he is confident his administration can broker an agreement with ByteDance, the social media app’s China-based parent company.
item.dc:creator: Sarah Parvini, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Annoyed by YouTube autoplaying videos on Android? Sorry, it’s not a bug, it’s another experimental feature
item.description: YouTube is yet again exploring with new features, as Android users have noticed this strange auto-play function in their mobile apps.
item.dc:creator: rowan.davies@futurenet.com (Rowan Davies), TechRadar

item.title: Nasdaq 100’s worst quarter in years sealed by AI bubble fears
item.description: In a quarter marred by tariff uncertainty, US government spending cuts and the threat of recession, it is fears about a bubble brewing in artificial intelligence that have dealt the latest blow to the Nasdaq 100.
item.dc:creator: Bloomberg, The Mercury News

item.title: Modern app delivery requires a continuous approach to security
item.description: At the Tanzu Division of Broadcom, we focus on how our customers can get the most out of cloud native environments while protecting against the slew of new vulnerabilities and attacks targeting their critical business apps.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: How to use Apple’s Mail categorization AI in macOS 15.4
item.description: If you are among the rapidly-growing number of Mac users in the enterprise, you should know that Apple’s newly-released macOS Sequoia 15.4 is festooned with security updates, brings support for Apple Intelligence across most major languages (including to Europe), and introduces new AI tools for the
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Detroit production of Mozart opera turns its female characters into automatons
item.description: DETROIT (AP) — Audiences at the Detroit Opera House expecting their performance of “Cosi fan tutte" to begin with the overture may be surprised to hear instead a product launch from a tech company CEO.
item.dc:creator: MIKE SILVERMAN, Associated Press, KRON4

item.title: Cisco takes inspiration from Iron Man for its AI-driven platform engineer
item.description: When it comes to developing highly intelligent AI agents, one might not think of combining open systems technology and the theoretical super-logic behind a movie character, but Cisco’s Outshift development team is doing just that.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: GameStop's Nintendo Switch 2 trade-in deals have seemingly leaked ahead of pre-orders going live, with big savings when upgrading from an original Switch
item.description: GameStop's Nintendo Switch 2 trade-in deal has seemingly leaked ahead of the Direct Showcase tomorrow.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Could Nintendo Switch 2 get a Final Fantasy 9 remake? Fans are hoping so as the game's 25th anniversary website goes live
item.description: Final Fantasy 9 fans are hoping for a Nintendo Switch 2 remake as the PS1 classic gets a 25th anniversary website.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Copilot+ PCs Get Live Captions, Image Generation Capabilities
item.description: Microsoft has delivered new AI features for Copilot+ PCs, including live captions and image generation capabilities. The features are coming to laptops with AMD and Intel processors for the first time.
item.dc:creator: TechRadar

item.title: Cómo la Ley de Protección de Datos Personales definirá la privacidad de los datos en el mundo digital
item.description: En el estudio Prioridades de seguridad para 2024 de CIO.com, el 40% de los líderes tecnológicos afirmó que una de sus prioridades clave es reforzar la protección de los datos confidenciales.
item.dc:creator: CIO

item.title: Spider-Man 4's official title has been revealed – and it has ties to a controversial time in the Marvel hero's 80-year-plus comic book history
item.description: Spider-Man 4 is officially named Spider-Man: Brand New Day, and it's a nod to an incredibly divisive storyline in Marvel Comics.
item.dc:creator: tom.power@futurenet.com (Tom Power), TechRadar

item.title: Agentic AI won’t make public cloud providers rich
item.description: While major cloud providers, such as AWS, Google, and Microsoft are rushing to position themselves as leaders in AI development and deployment, the reality of agentic AI’s impact on public cloud growth may not align with their ambitious projections—at least, in my opinion. I’ll tell you why.
item.dc:creator: Info World

item.title: Prioritizing an enterprisewide cyber culture in 2025
item.description: The three keys to building a resilient cyber culture is demonstrating leadership commitment, continually improving and staying flexible. The post Prioritizing an enterprisewide cyber culture in 2025 first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: commentators, Federal News Network

item.title: 23andMe’s rise and fall, in four charts
item.description: Just months after it started trading on the Nasdaq in June 2021, the company was valued as high as $5.8 billion. As of Friday, the firm’s shares were worth less than a dollar.
item.dc:creator: CNN.com Wire Service, The Mercury News

item.title: After fake employees, fake enterprises are next hiring threat to corporate data
item.description: Chinese companies are trying to cut Taiwan’s lead in semiconductor technology by hiring away its best engineering talent through ‘front’ companies that hide their connections to China, the Taiwanese Ministry of Justice Investigation Bureau (MJIB) has alleged.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Scientists hope a newly discovered flower will return after rain in West Texas
item.description: Scientists who want to learn more about a tiny, newly discovered flower in West Texas are hoping it will bloom again in a couple of weeks after rain finally fell in the area.
item.dc:creator: Jamie Stengle, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: AI megaprojects come with hidden costs
item.description: While Tesla’s stock has seen a sharp decline in recent weeks, Elon Musk has quietly been working to expand his influence in a different area of tech: AI supercomputing. Recent reports have revealed that Musk’s AI startup, xAI, has been quietly building a new data center in Atlanta.
item.dc:creator: Jason Ludwig, Fast Company

item.title: Stroke survivor speaks again with the help of an experimental brain-computer implant
item.description: Scientists have developed a device that can translate thoughts about speech into spoken words in real time.
item.dc:creator: Laura Ungar, Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: Microsoft adds data agents to Fabric to help business users with analytics
item.description: Microsoft is adding new data agents to Fabric, its cloud-based offering combining data warehousing, data science, data engineering, and data analytics services. Its goal is to help business users find insights in enterprise data without the help of a business analyst.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: Jack Black, Jason Momoa enter the Minecraft universe as the video game phenom hits the big screen
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — When Jack Black got the call to star in the “Minecraft” movie, the perks were plenty. He got to work alongside Jason Momoa, reunite with “Nacho Libre” director Jared Hess and engulf himself into a beloved gaming universe.
item.dc:creator: JONATHAN LANDRUM Jr., Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Elon Musk tries to rehab DOGE after backlash, mistakes
item.description: Elon Musk is pushing to improve the image of his Department of Government Efficiency (DOGE), suggesting the Tesla CEO is aware of the boogeyman persona he has created for himself and his cost-cutting push since joining President Trump’s team.
item.dc:creator: Julia Shapero, The Hill

item.title: Elon Musk’s xAI startup just bought X for $45 billion
item.description: Elon Musk said on Friday that his xAI has acquired X, the social media app formerly known as Twitter, in an all-stock transaction for $45 billion, including $12 billion in debt. “xAI and X’s futures are intertwined.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, Fast Company

item.title: OpenAI’s Studio Ghibli-style images renew the debate over AI and copyright
item.description: This week, the internet had a rare opportunity to transform its selfies and family photos into stunning Studio Ghibli “portraits.” What started as a lighthearted trend quickly took a darker turn.
item.dc:creator: Eve Upton-Clark, Fast Company

item.title: Facebook’s new throwback move: a feed just for friends
item.description: Meta wants to revive Facebook’s old-school appeal by introducing a new Friends Tab, which will focus solely on posts shared by a user’s friends and family. This Friends Tab will replace the section in the app that previously displayed friend requests and suggested friends.
item.dc:creator: Jessica Bursztynsky, Fast Company

item.title: How the Navy is removing barriers to IT modernization
item.description: Barry Tanner, the deputy CIO for the Department of the Navy, said the initiative to reduce and eliminate duplicative applications is targeting business systems. The post How the Navy is removing barriers to IT modernization first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Jason Miller, Federal News Network

item.title: Group chats rule the world
item.description: How many group chats are you involved in? For me, it varies. Right now, there are only two — and both are of a more private nature.
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: This may be the most private and secure cloud-based AI solution around
item.description: What if you could build a business-specific generative AI (genAI) solution that was hosted in the cloud? What if that cloud used the most energy-efficient systems in the world? And what if those services were also all running on the world’s most secure computing platform, the Mac? Wonder no more, b
item.dc:creator: Computer World

item.title: DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Hypori’s Jared Shepard on implementing zero trust security on mobile devices
item.description: The Hypori CEO details cloud approach and tactics for using mobile devices for secure collaboration. The post DoD Modernization Exchange 2025: Hypori’s Jared Shepard on implementing zero trust security on mobile devices first appeared on Federal News Network.
item.dc:creator: Tom Temin, Federal News Network

item.title: ChatGPT's viral Studio Ghibli-style images highlight AI copyright concerns
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — Fans of Studio Ghibli, the famed Japanese animation studio behind “Spirited Away” and other beloved movies, were delighted this week when a new version of ChatGPT let them transform popular internet memes or personal photos into the distinct style of Ghibli founder Hayao Miyazaki.
item.dc:creator: MATT O'BRIEN and SARAH PARVINI, Associated Press, ABC 4

item.title: Airtel connects India with 100Tbps submarine cable
item.description: In a significant development for digital infrastructure in India, Bharti Airtel has landed the 2Africa Pearls submarine cable, delivering 100 Terabits per second of international network capacity for the country. The submarine cable will connect to Airtel’s cable landing station (CLS) in Mumbai.
item.dc:creator: Network World

item.title: An AI-based drone that speeds up the detection and monitoring of wildfires is presented in Germany
item.description: EBERSWALDE, Germany (AP) — A company that specializes in early wildfire detection has developed a new, AI-based drone it says will help speed up the detection, location and monitoring of fires.
item.dc:creator: PIETRO DE CRISTOFARO, Associated Press, KLAS, CBS News

item.title: Los Angeles sheriff's department to retest 4,000 DNA samples after using faulty kits for 8 months
item.description: LOS ANGELES (AP) — The Los Angeles County Sheriff’s Department said this week it will retest 4,000 DNA samples and open an internal investigation after learning that it used potentially flawed test kits for eight months.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, Fox 5

item.title: Figure skating embraces the future with new technology, modern concepts to engage athletes and fans
item.description: BOSTON (AP) — Ashley Wagner and Ben Agosto know what it's like to step off the ice at the figure skating world championships and feel as though they've conquered the sport. And what it feels like when the notoriously fickle sport bites back.
item.dc:creator: DAVE SKRETTA, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Red Hat’s AI portfolio evolves to address enterprise deployment challenges
item.description: Red Hat announced updates to Red Hat OpenShift AI and Red Hat Enterprise Linux AI (RHEL AI), with a goal of addressing the high costs and technical complexity of scaling AI beyond pilot projects into full deployment.
item.dc:creator: Network World
